summary: Gruber shares a lightweight AppleScript he wrote to create new text files directly from the Finder's contextual menu. Inspired by the NuFile contextual menu plugin highlighted on TUAW, he wanted a simpler solution focused solely on text files. The script uses Brent Simmons's free Big Cat Scripts Plugin to add a contextual menu item that prompts for a filename, creates the file, and opens it in your preferred text editor. He provides instructions for adapting the script to work with BBEdit, TextWrangler, TextMate, or SubEthaEdit.
key_points: Gruber built a minimal AppleScript alternative to the NuFile plugin, focused only on creating text files
The script leverages Brent Simmons's free Big Cat Scripts Plugin for contextual menu integration
It includes basic safety checks like verifying the file doesn't already exist before creating it
The script is easily customizable for different text editors by swapping the command-line tool name
money_quotes: I thought that sounded pretty cool, but I'd only ever use it for creating text files.
The `do shell script` line at the end of the script assumes that your favorite text editor has a command-line tool, and that the tool is available in your shell's `PATH`.
key_insight: A simple AppleScript paired with a free contextual menu plugin can fill a small but real gap in the Finder's workflow for text-file-centric users.
